Class Workout

java.lang.Object
hr.unizd.workoutlog.model.Workout
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Workout extends Object implements Serializable
Represents a single workout entry in the WorkoutLog application.

This class is a model — it holds data about one workout session and protects the integrity of that data. Every required field is validated in its setter, and the constructor delegates to those same setters, so a Workout object can never exist in an invalid state (encapsulation).

It implements Serializable so that workout objects can be saved to and loaded from a binary file using Java's built-in serialization mechanism.

See Also:
  • Intensity
  • Serialized Form

  • Field Details

    • serialVersionUID

      private static final long serialVersionUID
      Version identifier for serialization. If we change the class in the future, updating this number prevents the application from silently reading corrupt data from old save files.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • exerciseName

      private String exerciseName
      Name of the exercise (e.g. "Running", "Push-ups").

    • durationMinutes

      private int durationMinutes
      Duration of the workout in minutes.

    • dayOfWeek

      private String dayOfWeek
      Day of the week when the workout took place (e.g. "Monday").

    • intensity

      private Intensity intensity
      Intensity level of the workout.

    • cardio

      private boolean cardio
      True if this workout includes a cardio component.

    • strength

      private boolean strength
      True if this workout includes a strength component.

    • note

      private String note
      Optional free-text note about the workout.

  • Constructor Details

    • Workout

      public Workout(String exerciseName, int durationMinutes, String dayOfWeek, Intensity intensity, boolean cardio, boolean strength, String note)
      Creates a new Workout with all required information.

      The constructor does not assign the fields directly — it calls the validating setters, so the exact same rules apply whether a value is set at construction time or changed later.

      Parameters:
      exerciseName - name of the exercise (must not be empty)
      durationMinutes - duration in minutes (must be greater than zero)
      dayOfWeek - day of the week (must not be empty)
      intensity - intensity level (must not be null)
      cardio - true if this workout has a cardio component
      strength - true if this workout has a strength component
      note - optional note; may be empty, but not null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any value breaks a validation rule

  • Method Details

    • getExerciseName

      public String getExerciseName()
      Returns the name of the exercise.
      Returns:
      exercise name

    • setExerciseName

      public void setExerciseName(String exerciseName)
      Sets the exercise name.
      Parameters:
      exerciseName - name of the exercise (must not be null or blank)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the name is null or empty

    • getDurationMinutes

      public int getDurationMinutes()
      Returns the duration of the workout in minutes.
      Returns:
      duration in minutes

    • setDurationMinutes

      public void setDurationMinutes(int durationMinutes)
      Sets the workout duration in minutes.
      Parameters:
      durationMinutes - duration in minutes (must be greater than zero)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the duration is zero or negative

    • getDayOfWeek

      public String getDayOfWeek()
      Returns the day of the week when the workout took place.
      Returns:
      day of the week (e.g. "Monday")

    • setDayOfWeek

      public void setDayOfWeek(String dayOfWeek)
      Sets the day of the week.
      Parameters:
      dayOfWeek - day of the week (must not be null or blank)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the day is null or empty

    • getIntensity

      public Intensity getIntensity()
      Returns the intensity level of the workout.
      Returns:
      intensity level
      See Also:
      • Intensity

    • setIntensity

      public void setIntensity(Intensity intensity)
      Sets the intensity level.
      Parameters:
      intensity - intensity level (must not be null)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the intensity is null

    • isCardio

      public boolean isCardio()
      Returns whether this workout includes a cardio component.
      Returns:
      true if cardio

    • setCardio

      public void setCardio(boolean cardio)
      Sets the cardio flag.
      Parameters:
      cardio - true if this workout has a cardio component

    • isStrength

      public boolean isStrength()
      Returns whether this workout includes a strength component.
      Returns:
      true if strength

    • setStrength

      public void setStrength(boolean strength)
      Sets the strength flag.
      Parameters:
      strength - true if this workout has a strength component

    • getNote

      public String getNote()
      Returns the optional note attached to this workout.
      Returns:
      note text, or an empty string if no note was provided

    • setNote

      public void setNote(String note)
      Sets the optional note.

      The note is optional, so an empty value is allowed. A null is stored as an empty string so the rest of the program never has to guard against null when reading the note.

      Parameters:
      note - note text; may be empty, null is treated as empty

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a concise, human-readable summary of this workout.

      This is the text displayed in the JList on the right panel and written to the text export. The format is: 

        Running | Monday | 30 min | Medium | Cardio | Note: felt great
      The category part shows "Cardio", "Strength", "Cardio + Strength", or "None" depending on which boxes were checked. The note part is appended only when a note was actually entered.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      formatted one-line summary of the workout

    • buildCategory

      private String buildCategory()
      Builds the category label based on the cardio and strength flags. Extracted into its own method to keep toString() readable.
      Returns:
      category string: "Cardio", "Strength", "Cardio + Strength", or "None"
